description: Comprehensive developer experience improvements to the build/dev workflow: add convenience commands for docs, plugins, and MCP server; add clean command; add colored dev output with startup banner; add check:plugins; add storybook shortcut; add docs check command; improve root package.json ergonomics with missing aliases and cross-project shortcuts. Update all .md documentation files to reflect the new commands.

userStories.title: Add `clean` and `clean:all` scripts to root package.json
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want a `bun run clean` command so that I can wipe all build artifacts and start fresh without manually hunting for dist/ and tsbuildinfo files.
acceptanceCriteria: `bun run clean` removes all `dist/` directories and `*.tsbuildinfo` files under `platform/`, `e2e/`, and root (tsconfig.configs.tsbuildinfo, tsconfig.scripts.tsbuildinfo) but NOT `node_modules/`, NOT `plugins/*/dist/`, and NOT `docs/.next/`

`bun run clean:all` does everything `clean` does plus removes `node_modules/` across all workspaces, `plugins/*/node_modules/`, `plugins/*/dist/`, `docs/node_modules/`, `docs/.next/`, and `docs/.content-collections/`
The clean script is implemented as `scripts/clean.ts` and uses Bun APIs (Bun.file, Bun.$, etc.) ŌĆö not shell one-liners in package.json
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Create `scripts/clean.ts`. Accept `--all` flag via `Bun.argv`. Use `Bun.Glob` to find all `dist/` dirs and `*.tsbuildinfo` files under `platform/` and root. Also clean `platform/browser-extension/icons/` (generated by build:icons). Use `rmSync` from `node:fs` for directory removal (no Bun equivalent). Add `clean` and `clean:all` to root `package.json` scripts:
- `clean`: `bun scripts/clean.ts`
- `clean:all`: `bun scripts/clean.ts --all`

The `tsconfig.scripts.json` already includes `scripts/**/*.ts` so the new file will be type-checked automatically.

userStories.title: Add colored ANSI prefixes to dev.ts process output
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the `bun run dev` output to have colored prefixes for each subprocess so that I can quickly distinguish [tsc], [mcp], and [ext] output when scanning a busy terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: [tsc] prefix is colored cyan
[mcp] prefix is colored green
[ext] prefix is colored yellow
[dev] prefix is colored magenta
Colors use standard ANSI escape codes (\x1b[...m) ŌĆö no external dependency like picocolors
stderr lines use the same prefix color (not red ŌĆö the content itself may contain error info)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit `scripts/dev.ts`. Define ANSI color constants at the top: `const CYAN = '\x1b[36m'; const GREEN = '\x1b[32m'; const YELLOW = '\x1b[33m'; const MAGENTA = '\x1b[35m'; const RESET = '\x1b[0m'; const BOLD = '\x1b[1m';`. Update `pipeWithPrefix` and `pipeTscStdout` to accept a color parameter and wrap the prefix. Update all call sites to pass the appropriate color. The prefix format should be `${color}${BOLD}${prefix}${RESET}` so the prefix is bold+colored but the log content is normal.

userStories.title: Add startup banner and ready message to dev.ts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want `bun run dev` to print a clear startup banner showing the MCP server URL and status so that I know when everything is ready and where to connect.
acceptanceCriteria: After initial tsc compilation + extension build + MCP server start, a banner is printed showing: MCP server URL (http://localhost:{PORT}/mcp), extension install path (~/.opentabs/extension/), and a clear 'Ready' message
The banner uses box-drawing characters or simple ASCII art to make it visually distinct from log output
The PORT is read from `Bun.env.PORT ?? '9515'` ŌĆö same logic already in the file

bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes

userStories.title: Add root-level docs convenience scripts
userStories.description: As a docs author, I want to run `bun run dev:docs` and `bun run build:docs` from the repo root so that I don't have to `cd docs/` every time I want to work on documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: `bun run dev:docs` runs `next dev` in the docs/ directory
`bun run build:docs` runs `next build` in the docs/ directory
`bun run lint:docs` runs `eslint .` in the docs/ directory
`bun run type-check:docs` runs `tsc --noEmit` in the docs/ directory
`bun run check:docs` runs build + type-check + lint + knip + format:check sequentially in docs/, stopping on first failure
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Edit root `package.json` to add these scripts:
- `dev:docs`: `bun run --cwd docs dev`
- `build:docs`: `bun run --cwd docs build`
- `lint:docs`: `bun run --cwd docs lint`
- `type-check:docs`: `bun run --cwd docs type-check`
- `check:docs`: `bun run --cwd docs build && bun run --cwd docs type-check && bun run --cwd docs lint && bun run --cwd docs knip && bun run --cwd docs format:check`

Note: docs is NOT in the root workspace. `bun run --cwd docs <script>` should work because it runs from the docs/ directory which has its own package.json. Test it. If `bun run --cwd docs dev` doesn't work (bun may not resolve the docs project's deps), fall back to: `cd docs && bun run dev`.

userStories.title: Add root-level plugin convenience scripts
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want `bun run build:plugins` and `bun run check:plugins` from the repo root so that I can build and verify all plugins in one command.
acceptanceCriteria: `bun run build:plugins` iterates over all directories in `plugins/` that contain a `package.json`, runs `bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build` in each
`bun run check:plugins` iterates over all plugin directories, runs `bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check` in each
Both scripts are implemented in `scripts/plugins.ts` to handle iteration cleanly (not shell one-liners)
Scripts exit with code 1 if any plugin fails, printing which plugin(s) failed
Scripts print the plugin name before running each plugin's commands for clear output
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Create `scripts/plugins.ts` that accepts a `--check` or `--build` argument via `Bun.argv`. Use `readdirSync` on `plugins/` to find subdirectories, filter to those containing `package.json` (use `Bun.file(join(pluginDir, 'package.json')).exists()`). For build mode: run `bun install --frozen-lockfile` then `bun run build` in each plugin dir. For check mode: run `bun run type-check`, `bun run lint`, `bun run format:check`. Use `Bun.spawn` with inherited stdio for output. Track failures and exit with code 1 if any plugin failed, printing summary. Add to root `package.json`:
- `build:plugins`: `bun scripts/plugins.ts --build`
- `check:plugins`: `bun scripts/plugins.ts --check`

userStories.title: Add `dev:mcp`, `build:tsc`, `test:unit`, and `storybook` aliases
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want convenient aliases: `dev:mcp` for just the MCP server with hot reload, `build:tsc` for just TypeScript compilation, `test:unit` as a semantic alias for `test`, and `storybook` to launch storybook from root.
acceptanceCriteria: `bun run dev:mcp` starts the MCP server with `bun --hot` and the `--dev` flag
`bun run build:tsc` runs `tsc --build --force` (just TypeScript, no extension bundling or icons)
`bun run test:unit` runs the same command as `bun run test` (alias for `bun test platform/`)
`bun run storybook` runs storybook dev in the browser-extension package
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Add to root `package.json` scripts:
- `dev:mcp`: `bun --hot platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js --dev`
- `build:tsc`: `tsc --build --force`
- `test:unit`: `bun test platform/`
- `storybook`: `bun run --cwd platform/browser-extension storybook`

These are all simple one-liners. `dev:mcp` is the same as the mcp-server's own `dev` script but runnable from root. `build:tsc` is useful when you only changed TypeScript. `test:unit` makes it clear what `test` does. `storybook` saves the `cd platform/browser-extension` dance.

userStories.title: Add `check` script to docs and plugin package.json files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `bun run check` to work inside docs/ and inside any plugin directory, matching the root monorepo's `check` pattern for consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/package.json has a `check` script: `bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run format:check`
plugins/slack/package.json has a `check` script: `bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check`
plugins/e2e-test/package.json has a `check` script: `bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check`
cd docs && bun run build passes
cd docs && bun run type-check passes
cd docs && bun run lint passes
cd docs && bun run knip passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run build passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run type-check passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run lint passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && bun run build passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && bun run type-check passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes:
Edit three files:
1. `docs/package.json` ŌĆö add `"check": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run format:check"`
2. `plugins/slack/package.json` ŌĆö add `"check": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check"`
3. `plugins/e2e-test/package.json` ŌĆö add `"check": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check"`

All three already have the individual scripts. This just chains them together. Plugins don't have `knip` so they omit it.

userStories.title: Expand `check:all` to include docs and plugins
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want `bun run check:all` to verify the entire repository including docs and plugins, so I have a single command that proves everything is green before pushing.
acceptanceCriteria: `bun run check:all` runs the root check suite (build, type-check, lint, format:check, knip, test, test:e2e), then runs docs checks, then runs plugin checks
If any step fails, the command exits with a non-zero code
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Edit root `package.json`. Update `check:all` to:
`bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check && bun run knip && bun run test && bun run test:e2e && bun run check:docs && bun run check:plugins`

This depends on US-004 (check:docs) and US-005 (check:plugins) being implemented first ŌĆö they have lower priority numbers so they will be done.

userStories.title: Sync plugin .prettierrc with root configuration
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the plugin prettier config to match the root config so that formatting is consistent when copy-pasting code between platform and plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/slack/.prettierrc has `bracketSameLine: true` and `htmlWhitespaceSensitivity: "strict"` added (matching root)
plugins/e2e-test/.prettierrc has `bracketSameLine: true` and `htmlWhitespaceSensitivity: "strict"` added (matching root)
The `plugins` array with prettier-plugin-tailwindcss is NOT added (plugins don't have that dependency)
No formatting changes are needed in plugin source files after adding these fields (verify by running format:check in each plugin)
cd plugins/slack && bun run build passes
cd plugins/slack && bun run lint passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && bun run build passes
cd plugins/e2e-test && bun run lint passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `plugins/slack/.prettierrc` and `plugins/e2e-test/.prettierrc`. Current contents:
```json
{
  "trailingComma": "all",
  "semi": true,
  "singleQuote": true,
  "arrowParens": "avoid",
  "printWidth": 120
}
```

Add `bracketSameLine` and `htmlWhitespaceSensitivity` to match root. Do NOT add `plugins` array since plugins don't have `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` installed. After editing, run `cd plugins/slack && bun run format` and `cd plugins/e2e-test && bun run format` to reformat any files. Then verify `format:check` passes.

userStories.notes:
This is a verification-only story. Run all new commands to confirm they work:
1. `bun run clean` ŌĆö removes dist/ dirs and tsbuildinfo files, then `bun run build` to rebuild
2. `bun run build:tsc` ŌĆö standalone TypeScript compilation
3. `bun run test:unit` ŌĆö alias for test
4. `bun run dev:mcp` ŌĆö start MCP server, verify it starts (Ctrl+C after seeing the port bind)
5. Verify `bun run check` runs the full suite
6. Verify `bun run check:all` includes docs and plugins
7. Spot-check .md files: grep for stale command references (e.g. old command names that were removed, or sections that should mention new commands but don't)

If any command fails or any documentation is inconsistent, fix it. This story's job is to ensure the entire batch of work is coherent.
